title: ibidium beckii
intro: Ibidium beckii, also known as the "devil's claw" or "grapple plant", is a woody vine native to southern Africa that belongs to the family Pedaliaceae. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including pain, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its medicinal properties which include antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ibidium beckii include relief from arthritis, fibromyalgia, and other musculoskeletal conditions, as well as improved digestion and reduced stress levels. Herbal preparations of Ibidium beckii include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be made from the plant's roots, leaves, or stems.
